# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 11

This is turn 11. Turns 1 through 10 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 11.

## Turn 11 — the determinant wall reduces to one Arf bit

I retain Candidate \(A_2\) unchanged. The attached history ended with only the \(E_\infty^{0,2}\) determinant-type classes over self-dual simple modules unresolved.  The exponent remains the prescribed \(\omega_2=40491355905\). 

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The implicit relation remains \(\tau^\sigma=\tau^2\).

### 1. Determinant classes cannot survive a nonsplit simple Frattini layer

Consider an extension

\[
1\longrightarrow V\longrightarrow E\longrightarrow G\longrightarrow1
\]

where \(V\) is a nontrivial simple \(\mathbf F_2[G]\)-module, and let

\[
\eta\in H^2(G,V)
\]

be its extension class.

The Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre spectral sequence has

\[
E_2^{p,q}=H^p\!\left(G,H^q(V,\mathbf F_2)\right).
\]

Since

\[
H^\ast(V,\mathbf F_2)=\operatorname{Sym}(V^\vee),
\]

the relevant differential is

\[
d_2^{0,2}:
H^2(V,\mathbf F_2)^G
\longrightarrow H^2(G,V^\vee).
\]

Let \(B\in(\Lambda^2V^\vee)^G\) be the alternating form underlying an invariant quadratic class. The transgression is

\[
\boxed{\quad d_2(B)=B^\flat_\ast(\eta),\quad}
\tag{11.1}
\]

where \(B^\flat:V\to V^\vee\) sends \(v\) to \(B(v,-)\).

Indeed, \(d_2\) is a derivation and, for \(\lambda\in V^\vee\),

\[
d_2(\lambda)=\lambda_\ast(\eta).
\]

Applying the Leibniz rule to a sum of monomials \(\lambda_i\mu_i\) gives exactly contraction of \(\eta\) by \(B\). Square terms contribute nothing because

\[
d_2(\lambda^2)=2\lambda\,d_2(\lambda)=0.
\]

Furthermore, the invariant square submodule is zero because

\[
(V^\vee)^G=0
\]

for nontrivial simple \(V\).

Every nonzero invariant alternating form on a simple module is nondegenerate: its radical is a \(G\)-submodule. Hence \(B^\flat\) is an isomorphism. Consequently,

\[
\eta\neq0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
d_2(B)\neq0.
\]

A Frattini extension is nonsplit, so \(\eta\neq0\). Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
E_\infty^{0,2}=0
\text{ for every nonsplit nontrivial simple Frattini layer.}
}
\tag{11.2}
\]

Equivalently, the restriction map

\[
H^2(E,\mathbf F_2)\longrightarrow H^2(V,\mathbf F_2)
\]

is zero.

This removes the apparent higher-dimensional analogue of the determinant obstruction from every genuinely deeper nontrivial Frattini layer. Such central classes are necessarily either:

- inflated from \(H^2(G,\mathbf F_2)\), or
- filtration-one classes in \(H^1(G,V^\vee)\).

The first kind reduces to a central extension of the smaller quotient \(G\). The second kind already has the exact \(50{:}50\) obstruction split proved in Turn 10. Thus **every central layer immediately above a nonsplit nontrivial simple Frattini layer is now closed uniformly**.

As a finite check, I reconstructed

\[
J=(C_4\times C_4)\rtimes C_3\longrightarrow A_4
\]

with kernel \(V=C_2^2\). Its extension cocycle is nonzero in \(H^2(A_4,V)\), while an exact normalized-bar computation gives

\[
\dim H^2(J,\mathbf F_2)=1
\]

and the restriction of every \(J\)-cocycle to all three coordinates

\[
x^2,\qquad y^2,\qquad xy
\]

of \(H^2(V,\mathbf F_2)\) is cohomologically zero, exactly as (11.2) predicts.

### 2. Only split elementary-core determinant classes remain

The only surviving determinant problem occurs when the elementary layer is split:

\[
E=V\rtimes H,
\]

with \(H\) a finite tame quotient and \(V\) a nontrivial self-dual simple module.

Let

\[
q:V\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2
\]

be an \(H\)-invariant nonsingular quadratic form, with polar form

\[
B(v,w)=q(v+w)+q(v)+q(w).
\]

It defines the extraspecial central extension of \(V\) under consideration.

Fix a surjective tame map to \(H\). Turn 10 identified

\[
H^1(\Gamma_{A_2},V)\simeq V
\]

by evaluation on \(x_0\). A class \(c\in V\) has a representative with cocycle values

\[
x_0\mapsto c,\qquad
\sigma,\tau,x_1\mapsto0.
\]

Put \(S,T\) for the actions of \(\sigma,\tau\), and let

\[
P=1+T+\cdots+T^{e-1}
\]

be the tame-inertia projector. Simplicity gives \(P=1\) when \(T=1\), and \(P=0\) otherwise. Let

\[
S_2=S^{\omega_2}.
\]

Evaluating the actual \(A_2\) word in the extraspecial extension gives:

- the \(x_0^2\) term contributes \(q(c)\);
- the \(V\)-coordinate of \(d_0\) is \((P+1)c\);
- the \(V\)-coordinate of \(z_0=x_0^{\sigma_2}\) is \(S_2^{-1}c\);
- the correction commutator contributes
  \(B((P+1)c,S_2^{-1}c)\).

Hence the complete obstruction is

\[
\boxed{
Q_{A_2}(c)
=
q(c)+B\bigl((P+1)c,S_2^{-1}c\bigr).
}
\tag{11.3}
\]

Thus

\[
Q_{A_2}(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,S_2^{-1}c),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{11.4}
\]

This is an exact evaluation of the candidate word, not just its polarization.

For \(V^T=0\), the polar form of \(Q_{A_2}\) is

\[
B_{A_2}(c,c')
=
B\!\left(c,\,
(1+S_2+S_2^{-1})c'\right).
\tag{11.5}
\]

Because \(S_2\) has 2-power order,

\[
1+S_2+S_2^{-1}
=
S_2^{-1}(S_2^2+S_2+1)
\]

is invertible: the minimal polynomial of \(S_2\) divides a power of \(X+1\), while \(X^2+X+1\) is coprime to \(X+1\). So \(Q_{A_2}\) is nonsingular.

### 3. The verifier count is determined by one Arf invariant

Let \(d=\dim V=2m\). For a nonsingular quadratic form \(Q\),

\[
N_0(Q)
=
\#\{c\in V:Q(c)=0\}
=
2^{d-1}+(-1)^{\operatorname{Arf}(Q)}2^{m-1}.
\tag{11.6}
\]

The class \(c=0\) gives a complement and hence a nonsurjective map to \(V\rtimes H\). Every \(c\neq0\) gives a surjection because \(V\) is simple. Each cohomology class has \(|V|\) cocycle representatives, and every liftable map has eight lifts through a central \(C_2\)-extension, since

\[
|Z^1(\Gamma,\mathbf F_2)|=2^3=8.
\]

Therefore, over each fixed surjective tame map to \(H\),

\[
\boxed{
\left|\operatorname{Sur}
\bigl(\Gamma_{A_2},\widetilde{V\rtimes H}\bigr)\right|
=
8|V|\bigl(N_0(Q_{A_2})-1\bigr).
}
\tag{11.7}
\]

So the entire remaining split determinant problem is reduced to comparing one bit:

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{A_2})
\quad\text{versus the true local Tate quadratic form.}
\]

For a 2-power-order isometry \(g\) of \((V,q)\), the Dickson–Arf identity gives

\[
\operatorname{Arf}\!\left(q+B(-,g^{-1}-)\right)
=
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(g-1)
\pmod2.
\tag{11.8}
\]

Applied to \(g=S_2\),

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{A_2})
=
\begin{cases}
\operatorname{Arf}(q),&T=1,\\
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(S_2-1),&V^T=0.
\end{cases}
}
\tag{11.9}
\]

Thus even the candidate-side obstruction no longer requires finite-group enumeration.

### 4. Uniform cyclic-character calculation

Let an irreducible self-dual cyclic block have character order \(d\), and put

\[
f=\operatorname{ord}_d(2).
\]

Self-duality is equivalent to

\[
f=2m,\qquad 2^m\equiv-1\pmod d.
\tag{11.10}
\]

Writing

\[
K=\mathbf F_{2^{2m}},\qquad K_0=\mathbf F_{2^m},
\]

every nonzero invariant quadratic form has the norm–trace form

\[
q_a(x)
=
\operatorname{Tr}_{K_0/\mathbf F_2}
\left(a\,x^{2^m+1}\right),
\qquad a\in K_0^\times.
\tag{11.11}
\]

Its number of zeros is

\[
1+(2^m+1)(2^{m-1}-1)
=
2^{2m-1}-2^{m-1},
\]

so

\[
\boxed{\operatorname{Arf}(q_a)=1}
\tag{11.12}
\]

for every nonzero invariant form.

For an unramified cyclic block, \(S_2=1\), and therefore

\[
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{A_2})=1.
\]

For the ramified normalizer, write

\[
f=2^a r,\qquad r\ \text{odd}.
\]

The operator \(S_2\) is the 2-primary Frobenius projection. Its fixed field has dimension \(r\), hence

\[
\operatorname{rank}(S_2-1)=f-r,
\]

which is odd. Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{A_2})=0
\quad\text{for every ramified self-dual cyclic block.}
}
\tag{11.13}
\]

Exact computations gave:

| character order \(d\) | \(\dim V\) | \(\operatorname{Arf}(q)\) | \(\operatorname{rank}(S_2-1)\) | \(\operatorname{Arf}(Q_{A_2})\) |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 3 | 2 | 1 | 1 | 0 |
| 5 | 4 | 1 | 3 | 0 |
| 9 | 6 | 1 | 3 | 0 |
| 11 | 10 | 1 | 5 | 0 |
| 13 | 12 | 1 | 9 | 0 |
| 17 | 8 | 1 | 7 | 0 |

For the corresponding unramified blocks, every nonzero invariant form had Arf invariant \(1\); the numbers of such forms were respectively

\[
1,3,7,31,63,15.
\]

### 5. The \(A_4/S_4\) conflict is now one instance of the formula

For the unramified two-dimensional \(C_3\)-module underlying \(A_4\),

\[
S_2=1,\qquad \operatorname{Arf}(Q)=1.
\]

A two-dimensional minus-type form has only the zero vector as a zero. Hence no surjective \(A_4\)-map lifts to the Schur cover, recovering

\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},SL_2(3))|=0.
\]

For the ramified \(S_3\)-realization underlying \(S_4\),

\[
\operatorname{rank}(S_2-1)=1,
\]

so the correction flips the Arf invariant to zero. The resulting hyperbolic form has two nonzero zeros. Formula (11.7) gives, per tame \(S_3\)-epimorphism,

\[
8\cdot4\cdot2=64
\]

surjections to \(GL_2(3)\). There are six tame epimorphisms, yielding

\[
6\cdot64=384.
\]

Thus the original \(SL_2(3)\)/\(GL_2(3)\) tension is not an isolated repair: it is the first case of the universal Dickson–Arf transvection law.

The exact bar and finite-field checks are reproducible here:

[Turn 11 transgression and quadratic certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn11_transgression_quadratic_certificate.py)

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved that every invariant determinant class is killed by the \(d_2\)-transgression above a nonsplit nontrivial simple Frattini layer; therefore no \(E_\infty^{0,2}\) obstruction survives in any such deeper layer. Derived the exact split-layer obstruction of the actual Candidate \(A_2\) word,
\[
Q_{A_2}(c)=q(c)+B((P+1)c,S_2^{-1}c),
\]
and reduced its complete surjection count to its Arf invariant. Proved the Dickson–Arf formula for the candidate and classified all cyclic-character blocks: unramified self-dual blocks have Arf \(1\), while ramified self-dual blocks are flipped to Arf \(0\). This uniformly recovers both the zero \(SL_2(3)\) count and the \(384\) \(GL_2(3)\) count.

**GAP**: The remaining substantive issue is now sharply isolated: prove that the true local Tate quadratic obstruction for every split self-dual simple tame module has the same Arf invariant as (11.9). The cyclic-character calculation gives the candidate’s uniform plus/minus pattern, but a local Hilbert-symbol proof is still needed for arbitrary semilinear scalar twists and endomorphism fields. Candidate \(A_2\) has not been run against the official 5402-group verifier.

**NEXT**: Compute the true quadratic form directly from the tame Kummer module \(K^\times/K^{\times2}\) and its Hilbert pairing. Use a normal-basis decomposition to prove, in one statement, that the local determinant form is minus type on unramified self-dual blocks and acquires exactly the Dickson invariant of the 2-primary Frobenius on ramified blocks.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
